title: conioselinum chinense var. pacificum
intro: Conioselinum chinense var. pacificum, also known as Japanese angelica or Angelica aciphylla, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, or it is used as a component in traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
